tips: Always provide fresh water alongside meals
Adjust portions based on activity level
Avoid sudden changes in diet - transition gradually over 7-10 days
Don't feed human food, especially chocolate, grapes, or onions which are toxic to pets
Establish a consistent feeding schedule

groomingVideos.title: How to Groom Your Dog at Home
groomingVideos.description: Learn the basics of grooming your dog at home including brushing, bathing, and nail trimming.

groomingVideos.title: Brushing Techniques
groomingVideos.description: Discover the right brushing techniques for short, long, curly, and double-coated dogs.

groomingVideos.title: Safe Ear Cleaning for Dogs
groomingVideos.description: Step-by-step guide to safely clean your dog's ears and prevent infections.

groomingVideos.title: Nail Trimming Without Stress
groomingVideos.description: Learn how to trim your dog's nails without causing anxiety for you or your pet.

groomingVideos.title: Dental Care for Dogs
groomingVideos.description: Important techniques for maintaining your dog's dental health through brushing and dental treats.

groomingVideos.title: Dealing with Mats and Tangles
groomingVideos.description: How to safely remove mats and tangles from your dog's coat without causing pain.

trainingTips.title: Basic Obedience Training
steps: Start with simple commands like 'sit' and 'stay'
Use positive reinforcement with treats and praise
Practice in short sessions (5-10 minutes) multiple times daily
Gradually increase distractions as your pet improves
Be consistent with commands and rewards
tips: Train before meals when your dog is motivated by food
Keep training sessions fun and end on a positive note
Use a clicker for more precise timing of rewards

trainingTips.title: Leash Training
steps: Introduce the leash indoors without tension
Practice walking in a quiet, familiar environment
Stop moving when your dog pulls and resume when leash is loose
Reward your dog for walking beside you
Gradually introduce more distracting environments
tips: Use a front-clip harness to reduce pulling
Be patient - leash skills take time to develop
Avoid retractable leashes for training

trainingTips.title: Potty Training
steps: Establish a consistent feeding schedule
Take your puppy out frequently (after meals, naps, and play)
Choose a designated potty area and use a consistent command
Praise and reward immediately after elimination
Supervise closely indoors and use a crate when unsupervised
tips: Clean accidents thoroughly with enzymatic cleaner
Watch for signs like sniffing or circling
Be patient and avoid punishment for accidents

trainingTips.title: Crate Training
steps: Introduce the crate gradually with the door open
Feed meals near and eventually inside the crate
Use treats and praise to create positive associations
Practice short periods with the door closed while you're home
Gradually increase crate time when you leave the house
tips: Make the crate comfortable with bedding and toys
Never use the crate for punishment
Ensure the crate is appropriately sized

trainingTips.title: Socialization
steps: Expose your puppy to various people, places, and sounds
Keep experiences positive with treats and praise
Introduce new things gradually without overwhelming
Arrange playdates with vaccinated, friendly dogs
Enroll in puppy socialization classes
tips: The critical socialization period is 3-16 weeks
Prioritize quality over quantity of experiences
Watch for signs of fear and don't force interactions
